Career Strategy

⏱ 4 min • Career

Long-term direction for AEM and DXP engineers.

📌 Summary
Long-term direction for AEM and DXP engineers.

Target product companies and GCCs that invest in Adobe Experience Cloud. Depth beats breadth: own the full delivery stack from authoring through edge performance.

Individual Contributor — AEM Engineer

Progress from component developer to senior platform engineer owning delivery pipelines and non-functional requirements.

  1. Foundation — HTL, Core Components, basic Sling Models, author/publish topology.
  2. Platform depth — Dispatcher, caching, OSGi services, Cloud Manager pipelines.
  3. Senior scope — Headless fragments, GraphQL, performance budgets, cross-team technical leadership.

Solution / Platform Architect

Shape multi-brand rollouts, integration patterns, and Experience Cloud alignment for enterprise programs.

  1. Integration patterns — AEM ↔ Analytics, Target, AEP; identity and consent architecture.
  2. Scale & governance — MSM blueprints, content supply chain, CI/CD and environment strategy.
  3. Modern experience stack — EDS, Universal Editor, composable commerce and personalization at edge.

Professional Branding

⏱ 3 min • Branding

Positioning and reusable messaging.

📌 Summary
Positioning and reusable messaging.

Position yourself as an Enterprise Digital Experience Engineer — not only an AEM developer. Use the templates below for LinkedIn, outreach, and resume bullets.

Resume bullet — AEM delivery impact

resume

Delivered [feature/migration] on AEM as a Cloud Service for [brand/program], improving [metric: e.g. publish lead time / cache hit ratio / Core Web Vitals] by [X%] through [Dispatcher tuning / pipeline automation / component refactor].

Resume bullet — headless / GraphQL

resume

Implemented headless content APIs using AEM Content Fragments and persisted GraphQL queries, enabling [web/mobile] teams to ship [feature] without duplicate content entry.

STAR story frame — production incident

interview

Situation: [outage/symptom]. Task: [your role]. Action: [triage steps, collaboration, fix]. Result: [restored SLA, post-mortem, preventive change].

LinkedIn summary pattern — DXP engineer

branding

Enterprise Digital Experience Engineer specializing in AEM [Sites/Assets/Cloud] and [Edge/headless/personalization]. I help product and marketing teams ship faster, safer content on Adobe Experience Cloud — background in [Java/frontend/platform] and [industry].

Recruiter outreach — AEM roles

networking

Hi [Name], I am an AEM [level] engineer with experience in [Cloud Service / Dispatcher / headless]. I am exploring [company/sector] roles where Adobe Experience Manager is central to the stack. Open to a brief call if you are hiring for [team/location].

Core Skills

⏱ 5 min • Skills

Priority technologies for senior AEM engineers.

📌 Summary
Priority technologies for senior AEM engineers.

Master the intersection of content platform engineering (AEM Sites, Assets, Cloud Service), integration (Analytics, Target, AEP), and delivery (Dispatcher, CDN, Edge Delivery). The technology reference table lists prerequisites and official resources.

TechnologyCategoryLevelSummary
AEM SitesCMSIntermediateEnterprise web content management: pages, templates, components, and multi-site authoring.
AEM AssetsDAMIntermediateDigital asset management, metadata schemas, renditions, and brand portals.
AEM FormsFormsAdvancedAdaptive forms, document services, and workflow-driven capture experiences.
AEM as a Cloud ServicePlatformAdvancedCloud-native AEM: rapid release cadence, Cloud Manager pipelines, and immutable containers.
Apache SlingFrameworkIntermediateRESTful resource-oriented framework underpinning AEM request processing and scripts.
OSGiFrameworkIntermediateModular Java services in AEM: bundles, configurations, and lifecycle management.
HTL (Sightly)TemplatingBeginnerHTML Template Language for secure, readable AEM component markup.
AEM Core ComponentsComponentsBeginnerAdobe-maintained component library with accessibility and Cloud Service compatibility.
AEM DispatcherCachingIntermediateApache-based caching and security filter in front of AEM publish tiers.
Edge Delivery ServicesEdgeAdvancedDocument-based authoring with edge-delivered pages and high Core Web Vitals scores.
Universal EditorAuthoringAdvancedVisual in-context editing across headless and hybrid experiences.
AEM GraphQLHeadlessAdvancedPersisted GraphQL endpoints over Content Fragments for SPA and native apps.
Content FragmentsHeadlessIntermediateStructured, channel-agnostic content models for headless delivery.
Adobe Experience PlatformDataAdvancedReal-time customer profiles, identity, and activation across Experience Cloud.
Java for AEMLanguageIntermediateSling Models, servlets, schedulers, and service users in AEM back-end code.

How I Apply

⏱ 5 min • Apply, Owner

My apply workflow, outreach, learning rhythm, and tools.

📌 Summary
My apply workflow, outreach, learning rhythm, and tools.

Personal methods below — opinion and workflow, not generic templates. Reusable resume and outreach templates remain in Professional Branding.

Apply workflow

Owner

Build the resume and align LinkedIn and Naukri before I apply anywhere.

  1. Build resume (ChatGPT or any agent): Explain your full experience — companies, technologies, education, projects, employment history. State what AEM role you want (backend, frontend, campaign, etc.) and what kind of companies you are targeting. Share your skills. Ask for a tailored resume with ATS keywords that highlight your profile for those roles.
  2. Do not use that export as-is. Go to Overleaf, pick a one-column resume template. Manually copy-paste content from the ChatGPT draft into LaTeX (easier than trusting ChatGPT-generated LaTeX — it often has errors). Fix any section that does not read right. Do not list GitHub, HackerRank, or other profile links unless you are actually active on them.
  3. Upload your Overleaf PDF to ChatGPT and a few free ATS checker sites. If the score is above 70, that is good enough to proceed.
  4. Upload the resume to ChatGPT and ask for steps to update LinkedIn and Naukri so they match the resume. Recruiters find you by keywords — skills and headline matter most. Share screenshots of each profile section and ask what to fill in (especially skills). Share your LinkedIn profile for a review. Spend real time on both profiles — they are mandatory. Use the same resume and profile details on any other job platforms. Update LWD, location, CTC, and other details accurately.

I do not start applying until the resume passes ATS checks and LinkedIn and Naukri are aligned with it. Profile quality matters as much as the PDF.

Outreach

Owner

Referrals, HR posts, and connection requests — not blind apply when I can reach a human.

  1. LinkedIn posts: Search AEM (or your role keyword), filter type to Posts. First pass — wider time window; after that — last 1 day only. Look for HR or hiring posts for your role. Often an email is in the post — send your application there. This works well because your profile goes directly to HR.
  2. Gmail cover-letter template: For LinkedIn Invites or Naukri invites, keep a reusable Gmail template — short cover letter with skills, experience, LWD if applicable, contact details, and resume attached. Reuse for every invite-style apply.
  3. Referral before direct apply: If I see a job on LinkedIn or Naukri, I do not apply immediately. I search that company on LinkedIn, find people in the same role (or close enough), and send a connection request. After they accept, I ask for a referral — not bluntly ("could you refer me?"). I use a ChatGPT-drafted template that mentions my role, experience, and why I fit. I wait for their reply. If referral works, great; if not, I can still apply later.
  4. Recruiter direct: If the post lists a recruiter email or contact info, reaching out there often beats a generic portal apply.

I treat outreach as higher signal than one-click apply. Templates live in Gmail; referral and HR-post wording I refine with ChatGPT once, then reuse.

Learning sources

Owner

Technical prep vs job-search prep — I keep them separate.

  1. Job-search content (resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, referral messages): ChatGPT or any agent — with my real experience pasted in, not invented bullets.
  2. AEM technical depth: This playbook's roadmaps, glossary, and interview prep — plus Experience League when I need to verify a topic before an interview.
  3. Employer research: Target Companies table and company row evidence before I interview or message someone at that employer.
  4. I do not claim tools or platforms on my resume or profiles unless I use them actively.

ChatGPT optimizes how I present myself; this playbook optimizes what I can defend in a technical conversation.

Weekly rhythm

Owner

How I search and apply on LinkedIn and Naukri after profiles are ready.

  1. First pass (one-time): On LinkedIn and Naukri, search your relevant role, set time filter to last 30 days, and apply only to postings that genuinely fit — batch this once when you start.
  2. Ongoing daily search: After the 30-day backlog is cleared, search the same role with last 1 day only — apply daily to new listings.
  3. LinkedIn posts: Search AEM (or your keyword), type Posts, wider window first then last 1 day on repeat visits. Check HR hiring posts and email applications (see Outreach).
  4. Platform hygiene: Same search strategy on LinkedIn and Naukri. Update your availability on Naukri at least every 15 days. Keep refreshing or updating the Naukri profile — activity improves search visibility.

Only after resume ATS pass and LinkedIn/Naukri are done. I combine portal apply with posts and referrals.

Tools

Owner

What I actually use for resume, profiles, and applications.

  1. ChatGPT (or any LLM agent) — resume draft, ATS feedback, LinkedIn/Naukri optimization prompts, referral message templates.
  2. Overleaf — one-column LaTeX resume template; manual paste from the draft, export PDF.
  3. Free ATS checker sites — sanity check before I publish the resume (target 70+).
  4. LinkedIn — profile, job search (30-day then 1-day filter), Posts search for HR hiring, company people search for referrals.
  5. Naukri — profile mirror of LinkedIn, job search with same time-filter strategy, availability updates every ~15 days.
  6. Gmail — saved template for cover letter + resume when applying via invite or email from a post.
  7. This playbook — Target Companies, filters, and interview prep once conversations start.

No apply bots. Every application uses the same polished resume and aligned profiles.

Glossary

⏱ 6 min • Reference

AEM and DXP terms used throughout this playbook.

📌 Summary
AEM and DXP terms used throughout this playbook.

Authoritative short definitions for enterprise digital experience engineering. Cross-links appear in the related-terms column.

TermDefinitionRelated
Adobe Experience Manager (AEM)Enterprise content management system for websites, assets, forms, and headless content. Core product in the Adobe Experience Cloud for digital experience engineering.dxp, aem-cloud
Digital Experience Platform (DXP)Integrated suite of products (CMS, DAM, analytics, personalization) used to design and deliver customer experiences across channels.aem, aep
AEM as a Cloud Service (AEMaaCS)Cloud-hosted AEM with continuous delivery, auto-scaling, and Adobe-managed infrastructure. Replaces self-hosted 6.5 for new programs.cloud-manager, dispatcher
Cloud ManagerAdobe pipeline tool for building, deploying, and monitoring AEM Cloud Service environments (dev, stage, prod).aem-cloud
Apache SlingRESTful OSGi framework AEM uses to map URLs to resources and scripts (JCR nodes).osgi, jcr
OSGiJava module system for AEM bundles: services, components, and configuration with hot deploy semantics.sling
JCR (Java Content Repository)Hierarchical content store (Apache Jackrabbit Oak in AEM) holding pages, assets, and configurations.sling
HTL (HTML Template Language)AEM templating language (formerly Sightly) that separates presentation from logic with automatic XSS context.core-components
Core ComponentsAdobe-supported AEM component library with versioning, accessibility, and Cloud Service compatibility.htl
DispatcherCaching reverse proxy and security filter in front of AEM publish; invalidation and filter rules are critical ops skills.cdn, aem-cloud
CDNContent delivery network caching static and dynamic edge responses; pairs with Dispatcher and EDS.dispatcher, eds
Edge Delivery Services (EDS)Adobe edge architecture for ultra-fast sites using document-based authoring and automated performance optimization.universal-editor
Universal EditorCross-surface visual editor connecting AEM content to any front-end experience.eds, headless
Headless CMSContent authored in AEM and delivered via APIs (GraphQL, Content Fragments) to SPAs and native apps.content-fragments, graphql-aem
Content FragmentStructured, channel-neutral content unit with defined models and variations for headless use.experience-fragments, graphql-aem
Experience FragmentReusable experience block combining layout and content for consistent omnichannel delivery.content-fragments
AEM GraphQLPersisted GraphQL API layer over Content Fragment models for efficient headless queries.headless, content-fragments
Adobe Experience Platform (AEP)Real-time customer data platform unifying profiles, segments, and activation across Adobe solutions.dxp, cdp
Customer Data PlatformSystem of record for first-party customer data used in personalization and analytics.aep
Adobe TargetA/B testing and personalization engine integrated with AEM and Analytics.analytics
Adobe AnalyticsDigital analytics product measuring content and conversion performance on AEM sites.target
Multi Site Manager (MSM)AEM feature to roll out blueprint sites to regional/live copies with inheritance controls.aem-sites
Live CopySite hierarchy synchronized from a blueprint with configurable inheritance of content and components.msm
WCM (Web Content Management)Traditional page-centric authoring in AEM Sites as opposed to headless fragment delivery.aem-sites
DAM (Digital Asset Management)AEM Assets capability for storing, transforming, and governing rich media.aem-assets
30 items · page 1 of 2

Interview Prep

⏱ 10 min • Interview

Technical and behavioral questions for AEM and DXP roles.

📌 Summary
Technical and behavioral questions for AEM and DXP roles.

Guidance-oriented preparation — frameworks and topics employers commonly assess. Not a guarantee of exact questions at any company.

CategoryLevelQuestionGuidance
technicalIntermediateExplain the AEM author vs publish architecture and typical deployment flow.Cover content activation/replication, Dispatcher on publish, and separation of authoring from delivery. Mention Cloud Manager for cloud deployments.
technicalIntermediateHow does the AEM Dispatcher cache and invalidate content?Discuss .stat files, TTL, invalidation requests from author, and filter rules. Note flush vs grace scenarios.
technicalBeginnerHow does HTL help prevent XSS compared to JSP scriptlets?Explain automatic context-aware escaping and separation of logic into Sling Models.
technicalIntermediateHow does Sling resolve a URL to a resource?Resource path, selectors, extension, and script matching. Optional: mention Sling Mappings.
technicalIntermediateWhat are OSGi configurations in AEM and how do run modes affect them?Factory configs, configAdmin, /apps vs /libs, and environment-specific run modes (author, publish, dev).
technicalAdvancedWhat changes when migrating AEM 6.5 on-prem to AEM as a Cloud Service?Immutable container, no custom runmodes in crx-quickstart, BPA reports, removed APIs, pipeline-only deploys.
technicalIntermediateWhen would you use Content Fragments vs Experience Fragments?Fragments are structured data for headless; XF are layout-ready marketing experiences for channels.
technicalAdvancedDescribe how persisted GraphQL queries work in AEM.CF models, persisted queries for caching/CDN, client apps vs ad-hoc queries in production.
technicalAdvancedWhat is Edge Delivery Services and how does authoring differ from classic AEM Sites?Document/sheet-based authoring, performance at edge, integration with AEM for governance.
technicalAdvancedHow do you diagnose slow AEM pages in production?Query performance (Oak indexes), Dispatcher cache hit ratio, component-level timing, Asset renditions.
technicalIntermediateHow do you implement a secure background job in AEM?Sling Scheduler or Job topics, service user mapping, error handling, idempotency.
behavioralIntermediateTell me about a time you unblocked a high-pressure production issue.Use STAR: situation, task, action, result. Emphasize calm triage, communication, and post-incident learning.
behavioralIntermediateDescribe a disagreement with an architect or product owner and how you resolved it.Focus on data, trade-offs, and alignment to customer impact — not winning the argument.
behavioralIntermediateHow do you balance delivery speed with quality on an AEM program?Automated tests, definition of done, incremental releases, tech debt visibility.
system-designAdvancedDesign a multi-country AEM site with shared components and local content.MSM blueprint/live copy, language copies, governance, translation connectors, rollout configs.
system-designAdvancedHow would you architect headless content for web and mobile from AEM?CF models, GraphQL persisted queries, SPA/SSR front ends, caching strategy, preview.
technicalAdvancedHow can AEM and AEP work together in a personalization use case?Edge data collection, Web SDK, segments, Target or AJO activation — stay high-level if not hands-on.
technicalBeginnerWhy adopt Core Components instead of only custom components?Maintenance, accessibility, upgrade path, Cloud Service compatibility, extension via overlay.
technicalIntermediateWhat belongs in an AEM Cloud Manager pipeline vs local development?Code quality, unit tests, RDE for rapid dev, full pipeline for stage/prod with approval gates.
behavioralBeginnerWhy enterprise digital experience engineering instead of generic full-stack?Tie to depth in CMS/DXP, business impact of content velocity, and long-term market demand.
